__warnings.note: 'Mhoro, {{name}}!' kept verbatim per design — it is Rwen's signature Shona greeting across all locales. The English 'What's on your mind today?' translated as 'आज तुम्हारे मन में क्या है?'. Reviewer: confirm 'Mhoro' should not flip to 'नमस्ते' for Hindi users.
'Ndine urombo' (I am sorry, Shona) kept verbatim per pack convention. The English 'something went wrong. Try again.' flips to Hindi. Mixed-language is intentional.
'Mhoro!' verbatim. The English 'I'm listening...' flips to Hindi.
Status labels include emoji — emoji kept in front, Hindi behind. 'Listening', 'Thinking', 'Speaking' translated as continuous tense.
'⏹' symbol kept; surrounding text translated. Reviewer: confirm 'दबाओ' (informal) over 'दबाएँ' (formal).

Used informal 'तुम' / 'तुम्हारा' throughout to match Rwen's warm-companion tone (matches French 'tu' decision). Hindi apps often default to 'आप' (formal). Reviewer: pick a register and ensure consistency across all 5 locale files.

input.placeholder_convo_active: बातचीत चालू है — बोलो या ⏹ दबाकर रोको

messages.welcome: Mhoro, {{name}}! आज तुम्हारे मन में क्या है?
messages.error_generic: Ndine urombo — कुछ गड़बड़ हो गई। फिर से कोशिश करो।
messages.auto_listen_greeting: Mhoro! मैं सुन रहा हूँ। जब तैयार हो, बोलो।
